statement: Instruction files covering C++ development are more effective when they treat testability as a design principle — code that is hard to test signals structural issues (hidden dependencies, side effects, mixed concerns) requiring design changes, not more complex test infrastructure.

evidenceRefs.title: r/cpp Design Patterns thread — eyepatchOwl: if hard to test, make it easier to test (design principle, not testing infrastructure problem)
r/cpp Software Architecture thread — darthcoder: good test cases make large structural refactors safe
C++ Core Guidelines — C.2: Use class if the class has an invariant; T.61: Do not over-parameterize

publicSummary: C++ instruction files should frame testability as a design principle: code that is hard to test has a structural problem that design improvements (dependency injection, pure functions, separation of concerns) should fix.
